text:
Once upon an autumn evening, a young fox decided to explore the dense woods nearby. His fur was as orange as autumn leaves. Along the way he stumbled upon a curious-looking old hat.

The hat was crooked. It didn't quite sit right. Maybe it belonged there. Maybe it would make an interesting hat decoration.

That night the fox took the hat home.

The next morning...

"Oh wow!" says the fox's owner. "That's the prettiest little hat."

It didn't look much like her. But maybe that wasn't such a bad thing.

Each day she wore it. People would stare. She liked that.

Years passed. She grew quite old.

She didn't mind much. Now she wore the hat.

The owl? A friend.

They traveled together. Many times. The owl loved tales.

That's how they learned...

The old fox loved feathers.

They flew away together.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
